name: Chicken Roasted with Basil, Garlic Butter and Lemon
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and pat dry the basil, reserving large whole leaves for the roasting pan and for the chicken cavity.
2. Mince the remaining basil. In a small bowl, blend minced basil, garlic, and butter with a wooden mallet or spoon (or using a mortar and pestle). Chill until slightly firm. This can be prepared ahead of time and refrigerated or frozen.
3. Rinse the chicken thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels.
4. With fingers, gently separate the skin from the meat, beginning at the neck and continuing down over the breast. Separate the skin through to the meaty portion of the drumsticks.
5. Place small bits of chilled basil butter between the breast and leg meat and skin, being careful not to tear the skin.
6. Halve the lemon and place one piece in the cavity of the bird, along with 3 or 4 whole basil leaves. Tie the chicken securely with string to ensure wings and legs are held in place.
7. Place the remaining basil on the bottom of the roasting pan; set the chicken, breast-side up, over top.
8. Drizzle the remaining lemon juice over the chicken and sprinkle with fresh ground pepper to taste.
9. Roast in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for 25 minutes.
10. Reduce the heat to 350°F (175°C) and roast for another 30 to 35 minutes, or until the drumsticks move easily in their sockets. Baste frequently with pan juices during this cooking period.
11. Let the chicken stand for 10 minutes before carving. Serve with defatted pan juices.
12. If you have leftovers, chop them and use for a chicken salad, sprinkled with toasted almonds or pine nuts.
